channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources

item.title: ⚓ Pentagon replaces Navy Secretary amid Iran blockade
item.description: US Navy Secretary John C Phelan is leaving "effective immediately" and being replaced by Undersecretary Hung Cao, with the Pentagon providing no explanation for the sudden leadership change during the ongoing Iran crisis.

item.title: 🇺🇸 Two Americans killed in Mexico crash were CIA officers
item.description: Two Americans killed in a vehicle crash in Chihuahua, Mexico on Sunday were CIA officers returning from a counter-cartel operation led by Mexican armed forces. The incident raises questions about US-Mexico security cooperation.

item.title: 🇲🇽 Migrant caravan leaves southern Mexico, many no longer US-bound
item.description: A migrant caravan departed from a southern Mexican city, but participants say many are no longer aiming for the US border, reflecting shifting migration patterns amid heightened enforcement.

item.title: 💰 Condom prices could surge 30% due to Iran war
item.description: Karex, the world's largest condom producer which supplies brands like Durex and Trojan, warns prices could rise 30% as the US-Iran conflict disruptpts global supply chains.

item.title: ⚖️ El Salvador holds mass trial for 486 alleged MS-13 members
item.description: El Salvador conducted a mass trial for 486 alleged members of the notorious MS-13 gang, with human rights groups warning that collective prosecutions violate due process and block defendants from accessing legal counsel.

item.title: ✈️⛽ Lufthansa Cuts 20,000 Summer Flights as Iran War Drives Jet Fuel Prices Higher
item.description: Germany's Lufthansa will eliminate 20,000 European short-haul flights this summer as jet fuel prices doubled since the US-Iran war began. The carrier is closing its CityLine service and canceling routes to 10 cities as the Strait of Hormuz disruption threatens Europe's fuel supply.

item.title: 🇹🇼🇨🇳 Taiwan President Cancels Africa Trip After China Pressures Countries to Revoke Flight Permits
item.description: President Lai Ching-te called off his visit to Eswatini after Seychelles, Mauritius, and Madagascar revoked his flight permits following what Taiwan calls "intense pressure" from Beijing. It's the first time a Taiwanese leader has had to cancel a trip due to revoked flight permits.

item.title: 🚢🇮🇷 Iran Seizes Two Cargo Ships in Strait of Hormuz as Blockade Standoff Continues
item.description: Iranian forces attacked and seized two vessels in the Strait of Hormuz, with one cargo ship reported as "heavily damaged." The escalation comes as Iran says reopening the strait is "impossible" if the US blockade continues, keeping the Gulf shipping chokepoint on edge.

item.title: 🇲🇩 Moldova's former powerful oligarch Plahotniuc sentenced to 19 years in prison
item.description: Vladimir Plahotniuc, Moldova's wealthiest businessman who de facto controlled the country's government in the 2010s, was sentenced in absentia. His fall is seen as part of Moldova's alignment with European democratic values.

item.title: 🇩🇪 Germany unveils strategy to become Europe's strongest military by 2039
item.description: The Bundeswehr will move away from rigid hardware quotas toward a flexible, effects-based planning model. The plan comes amid concerns about Russian military buildup.

item.title: ⚔️ Russia could be ready for NATO conflict a year after Ukraine, Dutch intelligence warns
item.description: Moscow's goal would not be to defeat NATO militarily but to divide it through limited territorial gains, according to a new Dutch intelligence report.

item.title: 🇨🇦 Canada PM hits back after Trump team demands 'entry fee' before trade talks
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney rejected US demands for concessions before trade negotiations could begin, pushing back against the Trump administration's approach.

item.title: 🇪🇪 Baltic nations brace for Iran war impact on US weapons shipments
item.description: Delays in ammunition deliveries could reopen a thorny question for Baltic HIMARS users: Would the US permit non-Lockheed rockets to be loaded?

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran Seizes Two Cargo Ships in Strait of Hormuz After Firing on Three Vessels
item.description: Iran fired on three ships in the Strait of Hormuz and seized two of them, escalating maritime tensions despite Trump extending the US-Iran ceasefire. The MSC Francesca and Epaminondas were taken to Iranian ports.

item.title: 🇪🇺 EU Approves €90 Billion Ukraine Loan as Druzhba Pipeline Restarts
item.description: The EU gave preliminary approval to the massive loan for Ukraine after Hungary dropped its veto following the restart of Russian oil flows through the Druzhba pipeline. The package includes the 20th round of Russia sanctions.

item.title: 🇳🇬 Nigeria Charges Six With Treason in Alleged Coup Plot Against President Tinubu
item.description: Six people including a retired major general and a police inspector pleaded not guilty to plotting to overthrow President Bola Tinubu. The case follows the cancelled Independence Day parade in October 2025.

item.title: ⛪ Pope Leo Condemns Prison Conditions in Equatorial Guinea, Calls for Justice
item.description: Speaking to 100,000 people in the oil-rich nation, Pope Leo criticized the treatment of prisoners and urged that the country's resources benefit all citizens, not just the elite. He is visiting a notorious prison in Bata.

item.title: 🇺🇸 US Justice Department Withdraws Subpoenas in John Brennan Investigation
item.description: The DOJ reversed course on subpoenas in the investigation of former CIA Director John Brennan, now requesting voluntary interviews. A Trump loyalist, Joe diGenova, was installed to lead the inquiry.

item.title: 🔌 NATO Launches First Response to Suspected Deep-Sea Cable Sabotage
item.description: A suspected sabotage attack on a deep-sea communications cable has triggered NATO's first coordinated response under its new critical infrastructure protection framework, marking a significant escalation in hybrid warfare tactics.

item.title: 🇨🇩 Rwanda-Backed Rebels Seize Congo's Humanitarian Hub City
item.description: Rwanda-backed M23 rebels have entered a key safe-haven city in eastern DR Congo that serves as a critical aid hub, raising fears for hundreds of thousands of displaced civilians and threatening regional stability.

item.title: ✈️ Italy Backs Saudi Arabia Joining UK-Japan Fighter Jet Program
item.description: Italian PM Meloni announced support for Saudi Arabia's participation in the Global Combat Air Programme (GCAP), signaling deepening defense ties between Gulf states and Western allies amid shifting regional dynamics.

item.title: 🌍 Extreme Weather Events Threaten Global Democracy, Study Warns
item.description: A new report finds that heatwaves, floods, and wildfires are increasingly threatening democratic institutions worldwide, disrupting elections and destabilizing governments in climate-vulnerable nations.

item.title: ⛪ Pope Leo Skips Major African Catholic Nations on Continental Tour
item.description: Pope Leo's Africa tour is drawing attention for skipping several of the continent's largest Catholic populations, raising questions about the Vatican's strategic priorities in the region.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran Ramps Up Ship Attacks in Hormuz After Trump Ceasefire Extension
item.description: Iran has intensified attacks on commercial vessels in the Strait of Hormuz despite the US ceasefire extension, raising doubts about the durability of any peace agreement. The attacks come as Tehran refuses to commit to substantive negotiations.

item.title: ✈️ USAF Doubles F-15EX Eagle II Order as Fleet Modernization Accelerates
item.description: The US Air Force has doubled its procurement order for the F-15EX Eagle II fighter jet, providing a ready successor to aging F-15E Strike Eagles. The decision reflects growing defense needs amid global tensions.
